task_yaml.task:
Our quarterly pipeline review is tomorrow and I need a clean sanity
check on the whole deals funnel before I walk in. Pull every row from
base $AIRTABLE_BASE_ID table $AIRTABLE_TABLE_NAME using $AIRTABLE_PAT
— use the workspace's Airtable integration skill for the data pull and
the database operations skill to crunch the numbers.

What I really want to see at a glance: which deals are actually stuck
versus just moving normally, where my people are over-loaded versus
not, which stage is the worst traffic jam right now, where the
dollar exposure is really concentrated, and which Won/Lost rows have
gotten so old we should treat them as cold and stop pretending
they're still in play. Whatever you decide to put in the writeup,
make sure those angles all come through.

Give me /tmp_workspace/results/audit.json with a quick health read:
- active_stuck_deals: every non-terminal deal in Lead, Qualified,
  Proposal, or Negotiation that has been sitting in its current
  stage for more than 21 days. List them all.
- moving_active_deals: all active non-terminal deals at 21 days or
  less.
- terminal_aged_deals: all Won/Lost deals older than 21 days, kept
  separate so they do not pollute active follow-up.
- stage_counts for all rows.
- active_pipeline_value_usd and terminal_value_usd.
- owner_followups: for every owner that has at least one active
  stuck deal, give me their stuck count, total stuck value, the
  oldest stuck deal id, the oldest days in stage, and a short next
  action. Each next action should start with an imperative verb
  like Email / Call / Schedule / Send / Submit / Review / Confirm /
  Escalate / Follow-up so it reads like a directive, and for owners
  whose oldest stuck deal is well past SLA you should call out the
  overdue duration explicitly with a day count, e.g. "45 days".

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/owner_followups.csv with columns
owner,active_stuck_count,active_stuck_value_usd,oldest_active_stuck_deal,
oldest_days_in_stage,next_action. For every stuck or terminal-aged
deal, include enough identifying info — deal_id, account, owner,
stage, value, days_in_stage — so I can chase it down without having
to crack open Airtable.

Please add /tmp_workspace/results/priority_queue.csv that lists the
most dangerous active stuck deals only — the ones with the largest
combination of dollars and time wasted. Compute risk_score as
value_usd * days_in_stage / 1000, rounded to one decimal, sort
descending by that score, and show me only the top tier of the
riskiest seven. Each row needs deal_id, account, owner, stage,
value_usd, days_in_stage, risk_score, and a short reason.

Feel free to weave the broader read-out into audit.json or a short
summary block in it so the picture across stuck deals, owner load,
stage flow, dollar concentration, and lapsed terminal accounts is
clear in one place — I want to skim once and know where to push next.
task_yaml.task_snapshot:
Our quarterly pipeline review is tomorrow and I need a clean sanity
check on the whole deals funnel. I've exported the deals to
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/airtable_snapshot.json — read from
there and run the analysis below over all the deals. Use the
workspace's airtable-pat-rest skill alongside the database-operations
skill so the audit follows the documented workflow even though you're
reading from the offline snapshot.

Honestly the thing I want most is a sanity check on the whole
pipeline. I want to see at a glance which deals are actually stuck
versus just moving normally, where my people are over-loaded versus
not, which stage is the worst traffic jam right now, where the
dollar exposure is really concentrated, and which Won/Lost rows have
gotten so old we should treat them as cold and stop pretending
they're still in play. Whatever you decide to put in the writeup,
make sure those angles all come through.

Give me /tmp_workspace/results/audit.json with a quick health read:
- active_stuck_deals: every non-terminal deal in Lead, Qualified,

- moving_active_deals: all active non-terminal deals at 21 days or

- terminal_aged_deals: all Won/Lost deals older than 21 days, kept

- stage_counts for all rows.
- active_pipeline_value_usd and terminal_value_usd.
- owner_followups: for every owner that has at least one active
  stuck deal, give me their stuck count, total stuck value, the
  oldest stuck deal id, the oldest days in stage, and a short next
  action. Each next action should start with an imperative verb
  like Email / Call / Schedule / Send / Submit / Review / Confirm /
  Escalate / Follow-up so it reads like a directive, and for owners
  whose oldest stuck deal is well past SLA you should call out the
  overdue duration explicitly with a day count, e.g. "45 days".

Also save /tmp_workspace/results/owner_followups.csv with columns
owner,active_stuck_count,active_stuck_value_usd,oldest_active_stuck_deal,
oldest_days_in_stage,next_action. For every stuck or terminal-aged
deal, include enough identifying info — deal_id, account, owner,
stage, value, days_in_stage — so I can chase it down without having
to dig through the snapshot.

Please add /tmp_workspace/results/priority_queue.csv that lists the
most dangerous active stuck deals only — the ones with the largest

Feel free to weave the broader read-out into audit.json or a short
summary block in it so the picture across stuck deals, owner load,
stage flow, dollar concentration, and lapsed terminal accounts is
clear in one place — I want to skim once and know where to push next.
